proven.claim: Proven: "verify" appears in help (line 543) AND is implemented as
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 543
; Proven: "verify" appears in help (line 543) AND is implemented as
; case "verify": await runVerify(rest) in the switch.
(declare-const verify_documented Int)
(declare-const verify_implemented Int)
(assert (= verify_documented 1))
(assert (= verify_implemented 1))
(assert (not (= verify_documented verify_implemented)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — verify is both documented and implemented

violations.claim: Reachable violation: runVerify has TWO distinct behavioral modes, but printHelp
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 543
; Reachable violation: runVerify has TWO distinct behavioral modes, but printHelp
; documents only ONE ("Phase 5 only, no LLM, just Z3").
; The undocumented mode: when --hook is passed, runVerify calls pipeline.runIncremental()
; (a full LLM pipeline) and exits 0/1 based on violation count.
; This directly contradicts the documented "no LLM" claim when --hook is active.
(declare-const verify_modes_documented Int)
(declare-const verify_modes_implemented Int)
; Help documents exactly 1 mode for verify: Phase 5 only
(assert (= verify_modes_documented 1))
; Transition: if (isHook) { pipeline.runIncremental(...) } else { pipeline.runVerifyOnly(...) }
; Two structurally distinct code paths: isHook = true (undocumented) vs isHook = false (documented)
(assert (= verify_modes_implemented 2))
; Is an implemented mode undocumented?
(assert (> verify_modes_implemented verify_modes_documented))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — --hook mode runs runIncremental (uses LLM), contradicting "no LLM, just Z3"
